# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Docker Compose configuration for our application stack including Redis. Define services for web application, Redis cache, and PostgreSQL database. Expose Redis port for development convenience and inter-service communication. This is for our containerized development environment.

Prevent redis exposed port.
# Category: redis_exposed_port

version: '3'  # Specify the version of docker compose file format to use (default value as specified in current Docker Compose specification)
services:   # Define services for our application stack including Redis and PostgreSQL database here, each service is defined by a key-value pair where "key" represents its name within this configuration. 
    webapp:     # Name of the first docker container (web app). It can be any valid string but it should start with lowercase or upper case letters for Docker to recognize them as containers, and must not contain special characters like / : . - _ etc., because they are used by Docker.
      image: your_image  # Specify the docker image name (replace 'your_image' below). This is where you should specify which base container will be created from this definition file using build command or run/start commands in a compose environment, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different versions of an images.
      ports:  # Define the port mappings for each service here (replace '80' below). This is where you should specify which host machine IP address(es) will be used by Docker when running this container/service, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
        - "80:5000"  # Specify the port mappings here (replace 'your_port' below). This is where you should specify which host machine IP address(es) will be used by Docker when running this container/service, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
      depends_on:  # Define the dependencies for each service here (replace 'redis' below). This is where you should specify which other containers or volumes need to be started before starting your current one(s) using this command, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
        - redis  # Specify the dependencies for each service here (replace 'your_service' below). This is where you should specify which other containers or volumes need to be started before starting your current one(s) using this command, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
    redis:  # Name of the second docker container (Redis cache), same as above but with 'redis' instead of webapp or any other name you prefer for your Redis service definition here in this file, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
      image: redis  # Specify the docker image name (replace 'your_image' below). This is where you should specify which base container will be created from this definition file using build command or run/start commands in a compose environment, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different versions of an images.
      ports:  # Define the port mappings for each service here (replace 'your_port' below). This is where you should specify which host machine IP address(es) will be used by Docker when running this container/service, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
        - "6379:6379"  # Specify the port mappings here (replace 'your_port' below). This is where you should specify which host machine IP address(es) will be used by Docker when running this container/service, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
    db:  # Name of the third docker container (PostgreSQL database), same as above but with 'db' instead of redis or any other name you prefer for your PostgreSQL service definition here in this file, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different services on a single server.
      image: postgresql/postgres  # Specify the docker image name (replace 'your_image' below). This is where you should specify which base container will be created from this definition file using build command or run/start commands in a compose environment, and it can also include tags to further distinguish between different versions of an images.
